assertions.description: Output references accessibility requirements (contrast, aria, keyboard navigation)
Output does NOT use hardcoded hex colors in component code
Output references semantic color tokens (not raw hex values)
Output references responsive design or mobile-first approach
Output references touch target minimum size (44x44px)
Output does NOT use emoji as icons (SVG icons required)
Output references animation/transition best practices (150-300ms, reduced-motion)
Output prioritizes accessibility rules by the skill's priority table (Priority 1-10)
Output length is under 700 words for focused responses

test_cases.input: Review this dashboard card component for UI/UX quality: it uses bg-[#0f4c75] for the header and has no aria labels on icon buttons.
Design a new settings page with form inputs, toggles, and a save button. What UX guidelines should I follow?
The mobile version of the CRM page has tiny buttons that are hard to tap. How should I fix touch interaction issues?
Full UI audit: review a landing page for accessibility, color system, responsive behavior, animation, and overall UX quality.
